{"key_event":"Collapse of the Somali state","reasoning":"The 1991 fall of Barre destroyed national coercive, fiscal, judicial, and administrative capacity, leaving only fragmented regional or factional authorities, so aggregate state power falls into the collapsed range despite Somaliland and Puntland institution-building. Civil society was badly damaged by war, famine, displacement, and warlord violence, but clan elders, merchants, Islamic courts, remittance firms, and northern/regional political processes kept significant autonomous organization alive.","society_change":-0.8,"society_power":3.7,"state_change":-3.5,"state_power":1.3}
